---
title: "\u200B"
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <meta
          name="description"
          content="Teaching page for an Assistant Professor of Accounting."
        />
engine: knitr
bibliography: references.bib
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero__content}
<p class="eyebrow">Arndt Weinrich | Teaching</p>


<p style="text-align: center"> <img src="/courses/54132/files/101696888.svg" width="75%" height="80"></p>

<div style="background-color: #171c54; color: white; padding: 10px;"> </div>

<br />

# <span style="color: #171c54;">Accounting, Analysis and Governance in Complex Deals</span>

Last Update: `r format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d")`


::: {.container}
::: {.card}
## Course Description

[Osiris/Sqill](https://eur.sqill.it/reports/163f2c25-9c25-41e4-bd81-8c1b9c3d667c){target="_blank"} contains important information on *Accounting, Analysis and Governance in Complex Deals* (BMME303). Please read all information carefully! Here's a sneak preview of the course description:

This course integrates accounting, financial analysis, and governance, providing students with advanced skills in understanding, assessing, and executing a variety of different transaction types, such as M&A, reorganizations, and other complex deals. Since such transactions often substantially transform businesses, understanding how to analyze and navigate these disruptions is essential for any professional involved in them.
:::

::: {.card}
## Learning Objectives 
- Understand how (financial) information creates and resolves conflicts between business leaders, oversight bodies, and other stakeholders throughout transactions.

- Critically assess complex multi-type and multi-source information in transaction-related valuations and decisions.

- Analyze the complex regulatory environment, including disclosure mandates, surrounding a variety of transaction types.

- Apply regulatory risk assessment frameworks, including commercial and tax due diligence, to transactions.

- Reflect on competencies in accounting, analysis, and governance acquired in other courses throughout the MSc programme to navigate complex deals.
:::



::: {.card}
## Course Instructor</span>

**Arndt Weinrich** | Assistant Professor of Accounting | [weinrich@rsm.nl](mailto:weinrich@rsm.nl) | [RSM website](https://www.rsm.nl/people/arndt-weinrich/){target="_blank"}

:::

::: {.card}
## Instructional Methods 

The course consists of **11 sessions** that combine elements of workshops and lectures. 

I expect interactive discussions to form the basis of our sessions, requiring students to be prepared, to reflect on previous input, and to actively engage with each other. Therefore, no livestreams or recordings are offered.

<div style="border-left: 4px solid #b62d7a; background-color: #C7C8CA; padding: 10px; margin: 10px 0;">
  <h4 style="margin-top: 0; color: #b62d7a;">Mandatory Attendance</h4>
  <p style="color: #171c54;"><strong>Mandatory attendance applies to guest lectures. These sessions are a core component of the course and provide essential content that cannot be replaced through alternative assignments or readings. Students who miss a guest lecture will be ineligible to participate in the corresponding weekly assignment (see assessment element "Assessment - Assignment"), resulting in a score of 0% for that week's assignment. Note that (i) assignments in total account for 20% of the final grade and (ii) students must pass all assessment elements to pass the course. Absences from guest lectures will thus directly impact your overall performance.</strong></p>
</div>

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

Tentative sessions for ***Guest Lectures with Mandatory Attendance***

- May 26, McKinsey & Company, Valuation   
- June 16, Boston Consulting Group, Synergies   

::: 

::: {.card}
## Schedule 

Check [timetables.eur.nl](https://timetables.eur.nl/link?timetables.0.key=BMME303&timetables.0.type=module&timetables.0.source=2025&timetables.1.key=BMME303_2025&timetables.1.type=timeedit_course&timetables.1.source=TimeEdit&ignoreEmpty=true&ignoreNotFound=true&view=month){target="_blank"} for an overview of appointments and room/lecture hall allocations

::: 

::: {.card}

## Mixed Form Assessment 

- "80% Element" &rarr; Final Exam (closed book) using Chromebook and Ans software

- "20% Element" &rarr; Assignments (think of weekly quizzes, open questions, and other deliverables, such as case solutions) throughout the course (may include peer feedback)

- Note that you must pass all assessment elements in order to pass the course. No carry-overs!   

- Expectations? Check the [assessment rubric](/assessment_rubric.html){target="_blank"} for detailed descriptions of expected performance at each level (Beginner, Developing, Accomplished, Exemplary) for every course learning goal

:::

::: {.card}
## [Canvas]{.bg}

- Canvas is back online after a week of downtime due to a cyber attack at the software provider ([EUR Update Blog](https://my.eur.nl/en/eur-employee/news/update-may-12-canvas-will-be-available-again-tomorrow){target="_blank"})

- I will continue to use [arndtupb.github.io](https://arndtupb.github.io){target="_blank"} as an alternative platform to share *some* important information and materials with you in case we loose access again

- [**Week 1 Quiz**]{.bg}
    - Goes online no later than May/15

    - [**Deadline to complete 20 multiple choice questions is May/18 at 11:59 PM**]{.fg} (Rotterdam Time)
    
    - Let's walk through more details at the [deadline vault](https://canvas.eur.nl/courses/54132/pages/01b-deadline-vault){target="_blank"}

:::


::: {.card}
## GameStop's Push to Acquire eBay

- On May/4, Gamestop's CEO, Ryan Cohen, expressed interest in acquiring eBay: a move that surprised many given the companies' different business models and market positions

- The next day, he gave an interview to CNBC which earned him the headline ***"GameStop’s Ryan Cohen is either the new Buffett or the anti-Buffett"*** [FT](https://www.ft.com/content/a18c4115-7178-456c-a7ff-4b0e7f9ec9aa?syn-25a6b1a6=1){target="_blank"} 

$\Rightarrow$ Watch the video on [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bmj2PaxX24E){target="_blank"}!  

- eBay rejected the bid, calling the offer *"neither credible nor attractive"* [NYT](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/12/business/ebay-gamestop-takeover.html?nl=dealbook&segment_id=219724{target="_blank"}) & [8K Filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065088/000155278126000322/e26249_ebay-8k.htm){target="_blank"} & [eBay Letter](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065088/000155278126000322/e26249_ex99-2.htm){target="_blank"}

- See this [FT Summary](https://ask.ft.com/answer/ccdffdbb-fd19-4870-ba54-c46dba6150d2){target="_blank"} for more details (updated May/13)

$\Rightarrow$ this (short lived) bid is a great example for accounting, analysis, and governance in complex deals, and we will refer to it throughout the course! Familiarize yourself with the case! 

::::



::: {.card}
## Kickoff and Introduction to Complex Deals (Week One S1)

You can download the slides as HTML and PDF. Note that slides in PDF format often lose some features (e.g., embedded tabs, videos, etc.) and are thus not the preferred format. 

- **Slides: Kickoff** [HTML](/courses/54132/files/intro.html){target="_blank"} and [PDF](/courses/54132/files/intro.pdf){target="_blank"}

- **Slides: Transactions** [HTML](/courses/54132/files/intro_transactions.html){target="_blank"} and [PDF](/courses/54132/files/intro_transactions.pdf){target="_blank"} & **FAQ** on Reading [Organizational Charts](/courses/54132/files/orga_chart.html){target="_blank"}

- **Slides: Governance** [HTML](/courses/54132/files/intro_governance.html){target="_blank"} and [PDF](/courses/54132/files/intro_governance.pdf){target="_blank"}

:::

::: {.card}
## Target Selection (Week One S2)

- We translate acquisition strategy into practical target-screening criteria 

- We connect value-creating archetypes to measurable financial indicators

- We discuss information asymmetry and governance risks in early target selection

- The OpenAI-Windsurf case is used as a stylized application of the screening framework

- **Slides Target Selection** in [HTML](/courses/54132/files/target_identification.html){target="_blank"} and [PDF](/courses/54132/files/target_identification.pdf){target="_blank"} 

:::


::: {.card}
## Literature 

- While there is no single literature source for the course, the following books are used as key reference materials:

  - @depamphilis2021mergers covers a wide range of topics (2019 version electronically available at [EUR](https://eur.on.worldcat.org/oclc/1124929442){target="_blank"})

  - @koller2010valuation is a classic in the field of valuation (2020/7th version electronically available at [EUR](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/valuation-7th-edition/9781119610885/){target="_blank"})

- Additionally, academic articles are used to illustrate the topics covered in the course and are exam-relevant (see slides)

*Reference List*

::: {#refs}
:::

:::


<hr />

<p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: #808080;"><em>Accounting, Analysis and Governance in Complex Deals</em></span></p>

<p style="text-align: center"> <img src="/courses/54132/files/101696886.svg" width="200" height="80"></p>

<br/>
